rationale: Both candidates miss all four core confirmed bugs (slot leak on abort, cumulative/delta re-slice, ceil-division length bug, legacy-checkpoint v2 dispatch), so recall is near-zero for both, with X getting a sliver of credit for a tangential load_weights/legacy-checkpoint fragility note. Critically, X asserts slot-lifecycle cleanup is 'handled' and vendored files carry 'proper' provenance headers — both directly contradicting the two highest-severity confirmed findings (slot leak, missing vendor provenance), which actively misleads rather than just omitting. Y avoids that severity of error (its one wrong validation is on a minor WER-fallback point) and its shared findings (max_new_frames TypeError regression, ignored talker_mtp sampling params, missing tests) are backed by explicit grep/line evidence, giving it higher precision and slightly crisper actionability. Neither review surfaces a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
